title: Autofluorescence Microscopy Data Acquisition
author: Elizabeth Neumann
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7e3hjgn
materials: Define the imaging region that includes the tissue.

steps.title: If sectioned tissue is frozen, return...
steps.instruction: If sectioned tissue is frozen, return to room temperature (~20 o C) within a vacuum desiccator (~30 min), otherwise proceed directly to step 2.

steps.title: Place microscope slide within adapter and...
steps.instruction: Place microscope slide within adapter and insert into the Zeiss AxioScan Slide Scanner

steps.title: Perform coarse focusing of the tissue using
steps.instruction: Perform coarse focusing of the tissue using: DAPI filter set (ex. 335-383 nm; em. 420-470 nm, blue) Lamp power (~90%) and moderate exposure times (~150 ms)

steps.title: Perform fine focusing of the tissue...
steps.instruction: Perform fine focusing of the tissue using the following to build a focus map: DAPI (blue) GFP (ex. 450-490 nm; em. 500-550, green) DsRed (ex. 538-562 nm; em. 570-640, red) Lamp power (~90%) and moderate exposure times (~150 ms)

steps.title: Export autofluorescence image as an OME-TIF...
steps.instruction: Export autofluorescence image as an OME-TIF with the following options: "BigTIFF" "Use Tiles" "Compress" "Convert to 8 Bit" Alternatively, other image file types, such as "BigTIFF" and "pngs" can also be useful, depending on the application.
